Title: Striga hermonthica induces lignin deposition at the root tip to facilitate prehaustorium formation and obligate parasitism

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates cinnamic acid to p-coumaric acid in an early step of monolignol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Monolignol biosynthesis via the C4H step is required for efficient HIF-induced lignification and prehaustorium formation; pharmacological inhibition of C4H reduces prehaustorium formation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: HIF treatment including S-DMAC, syringic acid, DMBQ, and peonidin; piperonylic acid treatment
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Chemical inhibition with piperonylic acid targeting C4H; RT-qPCR of lignin biosynthetic homologs; prehaustorium formation assay
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C4H is an early monolignol-biosynthetic step explicitly targeted by piperonylic acid in this study. 2. Inhibiting this step significantly reduced HIF-induced prehaustorium formation. 3. The data support endogenous monolignol biosynthesis as functionally required for lignification during prehaustorium development.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: prehaustorial epidermal cell wall at the root tip
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: C4H homolog(s) are among lignin biosynthetic genes significantly upregulated within 12 h of DMBQ treatment
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: early required step supported pharmacologically, but rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper does not identify a single specific ShC4H locus as the decisive core subject; evidence is pathway-level and pharmacological.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Produces apoplastic reactive oxygen species, including H2O2, that support lignification-associated oxidative processes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: DMBQ strongly induces ShRBOHJ expression during early prehaustorium formation, supporting H2O2 accumulation associated with lignification and development.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR time course; H2DCFDA and DAB staining; inhibitor assays with DPI and KI
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ShRBOHJ was the earliest induced RBOH, increasing >10-fold by 6 h after DMBQ treatment. 2. H2O2 accumulated at the prehaustorial epidermis during induction. 3. ROS inhibition reduced prehaustorium formation, indicating functional involvement downstream of HIFs.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: prehaustorial epidermal cell wall at the root tip
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced during early DMBQ response, peaking from 6 to 24 h
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: ROS inhibition suppresses development, but specific ShRBOHJ tradeoffs were not reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ROS and peroxidase-driven lignification appear to act in parallel; the paper does not provide direct genetic perturbation of ShRBOHJ.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Produces reactive oxygen species that contribute to H2O2 accumulation during prehaustorium formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induced by DMBQ during prehaustorium formation and associated with ROS accumulation downstream of HIFs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR time course; H2DCFDA and DAB staining; inhibitor assays with DPI and KI
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ShRBOHD expression gradually increased from 3 to 24 h after DMBQ treatment. 2. The induction coincided with increased H2O2 at the prehaustorial epidermis. 3. ROS production supports prehaustorium formation, though direct genetic validation was not performed.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: prehaustorial epidermal cell wall at the root tip
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Gradually upregulated from 3 to 24 h after DMBQ treatment

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Produces reactive oxygen species that contribute to H2O2 accumulation during prehaustorium formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induced by DMBQ during prehaustorium formation and associated with ROS accumulation downstream of HIFs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR time course; H2DCFDA and DAB staining; inhibitor assays with DPI and KI
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ShRBOHE was induced from 6 to 24 h after DMBQ treatment. 2. Induction coincided with epidermal H2O2 accumulation during prehaustorium formation. 3. Supports a role for ROS supply in HIF-triggered lignification-associated development.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: prehaustorial epidermal cell wall at the root tip
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated from 6 to 24 h after DMBQ treatment

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively polymerizes monolignols into lignin using H2O2 and likely promotes incorporation of external monolignols into the prehaustorial cell wall.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Facilitates prehaustorium formation by promoting lignification; heterologous expression increases prehaustorium number after DMBQ treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: DMBQ; syringic acid; rice infection; heterologous expression in hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomics; RT-qPCR; heterologous expression in P. japonicum hairy roots under PjABCB4 promoter; prehaustorium quantification; inhibitor evidence with SHAM
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ShPER52 is induced by DMBQ, syringic acid, and during host infection. 2. Peroxidase inhibition strongly reduced S-DMAC incorporation into prehaustoria. 3. Heterologous expression of ShPER52 in P. japonicum significantly increased DMBQ-induced prehaustorium formation, supporting a causal facilitative role.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: prehaustorial epidermal cell wall at the root tip
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In S. hermonthica, induced during prehaustorium formation and host infection; in transgenic P. japonicum, expressed in epidermis and stele under PjABCB4 promoter
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: cell wall/apoplast (inferred for class III peroxidase)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key facilitative polymerization enzyme, but no single rate-limiting status established
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target to suppress Striga prehaustorium formation or engineer anti-parasitic strategies by disrupting parasite lignification
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named by similarity to Arabidopsis PRX52/PER52; tested in heterologous parasitic plant system rather than by knockout in Striga.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively polymerizes monolignols into lignin using H2O2 and likely promotes lignification-associated prehaustorium development.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Facilitates prehaustorium formation; heterologous expression increases prehaustorium number after DMBQ treatment.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: DMBQ; syringic acid; rice infection; heterologous expression in hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomics; RT-qPCR; heterologous expression in P. japonicum hairy roots under PjABCB4 promoter; prehaustorium quantification; inhibitor evidence with SHAM
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ShPER2 is a HIF-responsive class III peroxidase induced during early prehaustorium formation and host infection. 2. Expression of ShPER2 in P. japonicum significantly increased prehaustorium formation after DMBQ treatment. 3. The results support a causal role in peroxidase-mediated lignification that facilitates prehaustorium development.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: prehaustorial epidermal cell wall at the root tip
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In S. hermonthica, induced by HIFs and host infection; in transgenic P. japonicum, expressed in epidermis and stele under PjABCB4 promoter
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: cell wall/apoplast (inferred for class III peroxidase)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential anti-Striga target through inhibition of parasite lignification machinery
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Named by similarity to Arabidopsis PRX2/PER2; validated in heterologous P. japonicum system.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Predicted class III peroxidase involved in oxidative monolignol polymerization during lignification.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate facilitator of prehaustorium formation, but heterologous expression did not significantly increase prehaustorium number in P. japonicum.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: DMBQ; syringic acid; rice infection; heterologous expression in hairy roots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomics; cloning and heterologous expression in P. japonicum hairy roots; prehaustorium quantification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ShPER7 was selected because it is induced by DMBQ, syringic acid, and host infection. 2. Unlike ShPER52 and ShPER2, ShPER7 did not enhance prehaustorium formation in the heterologous assay. 3. The authors note that this negative result may reflect system-specific limitations rather than lack of function in S. hermonthica.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: prehaustorial epidermal cell wall at the root tip
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in S. hermonthica by HIFs and during host infection
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: cell wall/apoplast (inferred for class III peroxidase)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function remains unresolved because heterologous P. japonicum may lack downstream machinery for ShPER7 activity.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes phenolic substrates with oxygen during lignin polymerization.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Only limited, condition-specific induction was observed; laccases were not broadly supported as common HIF-responsive drivers of prehaustorium lignification.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among 19 ShLAC genes, none were commonly upregulated by both DMBQ and syringic acid. 2. ShLAC7 was induced late by DMBQ and syringic acid. 3. The study does not support laccases as major core drivers compared with class III peroxidases.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: prehaustorial epidermal cell wall at the root tip
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced at 18 h by DMBQ and 12 h by syringic acid
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a minor candidate, but evidence is weaker than for class III peroxidases.
